patch.value: Per-command code-splitting: lazy command-module imports drop the 943KB monolith + fast-glob from the read path

Startup perf: the 4 register-* families lazily imported the entire commands/index.js barrel (~40 modules, 32K LOC) inside every action handler, so esbuild --splitting produced ONE 943KB commands chunk. Running any command (e.g. list) loaded all command code AND their deps — notably fast-glob's ~15-package transitive tree (braces/picomatch/micromatch/glob-parent/fastq/...) pulled in only by linked-artifacts.ts. Changed all 44 handler call sites to import the specific command module (import('./commands/<x>.js')), so esbuild emits a chunk per command and a command loads only its own chunk + shared core chunks. Verified: fast-glob resolutions during `pm list` 30+ -> 0; the 943KB monolith is gone. Dev-repo (best of 7): search 673ms->292ms, show 370ms->204ms, list 296-527ms->266ms. Companion to pm-3mal (completion.ts now only loads for `pm completion`).

Implemented and verified. All 44 handler call sites in register-setup/list-query/mutation/operations now import their specific command module; the unused barrel lazy-loaders were removed. esbuild now emits per-command chunks (verified: 943KB commands monolith gone, fast-glob 0 resolutions during `pm list`). Full suite green (2029 tests).
